row.openHint: Open a terminal in this worktree
row.focusHint: Go to the terminal already in this worktree
row.splitHint: Open it beside what you are working in
row.openStale: The directory is gone — nothing to open
row.pruneHint: Drop git's record of worktrees whose directory is gone

empty.repo: No worktrees in this repo yet
empty.repoHint: Create one from a terminal's ⋯ menu
empty.globalHint: Repos show up here once they have a worktree

create.title: New worktree

error.flag: A name cannot start with a dash — git would read it as an option
error.invalid: Git will not take this as a branch name

create.setupHint: Remembered for this repo. A fresh worktree has no node_modules.
create.copyHint: Gitignored files git does not bring along. Comma-separated globs.
create.copyFailed: The worktree was created, but the local files could not be copied: {{error}}

remove.title: Remove worktree
remove.message: Removes the directory {{path}}. The commits on this branch stay in the repo.
remove.dirty: {{count}} uncommitted change will be destroyed. Nothing else has a copy.
remove.dirty_other: {{count}} uncommitted changes will be destroyed. Nothing else has a copy.
remove.dirtyUnknown: Its uncommitted changes could not be counted, so anything there would be destroyed.
remove.discard: I understand this work will be discarded
remove.deleteBranchHint: Kept by default. Git refuses to delete a branch that is not merged.
remove.forceDeleteBranch: Delete the branch even if it is not merged
remove.main: This is the repo's own working tree, not a worktree you added. It cannot be removed here.
remove.locked: This worktree is locked, so git will not remove it. Unlock it first.
remove.stale: The directory is already gone. This just drops git's record of it.

pane.hintBody: Create a worktree here, or look at the ones you already have
